name: browser_click
description: Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

level.description: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

all.description: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

name: browser_drag
description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startRef.description: Exact source element reference from the page snapshot

startSelector.description: CSS or role selector for the source element, when ref is not available

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endRef.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

endSelector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when ref is not available

name: browser_evaluate
description: Evaluate JavaScript expression on page or element

function.description: () => { /* code */ } or (element) => { /* code */ } when element is provided

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available.

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available

name: browser_navigate_back
description: Go back to the previous page in the history

name: browser_network_requests
description: Returns all network requests since loading the page

includeStatic.description: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

name: browser_press_key
description: Press a key on the keyboard

key.description: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

code.description: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

name: browser_select_option
description: Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

name: browser_snapshot
description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

filename.description: Save snapshot to markdown file instead of returning it in the response.

selector.description: Element selector of the root element to capture a partial snapshot instead of the whole page

name: browser_take_screenshot
description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to screenshot the element. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If element is provided, ref must be provided too.

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot. If not provided, the screenshot will be taken of viewport. If ref is provided, element must be provided too.

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

name: browser_wait_for
description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear

name: browser_tabs
description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

name: browser_get_config
description: Get the final resolved config after merging CLI options, environment variables and config file.

name: browser_network_state_set
description: Sets the browser network state to online or offline. When offline, all network requests will fail.

state.description: Set to "offline" to simulate offline mode, "online" to restore network connectivity

name: browser_route
description: Set up a route to mock network requests matching a URL pattern

headers.description: Headers to add in "Name: Value" format

removeHeaders.description: Comma-separated list of header names to remove from request

name: browser_unroute
description: Remove network routes matching a pattern (or all routes if no pattern specified)

pattern.description: URL pattern to unroute (omit to remove all routes)

name: browser_cookie_get
description: Get a specific cookie by name

name: browser_cookie_list
description: List all cookies (optionally filtered by domain/path)

name: browser_cookie_set
description: Set a cookie with optional flags (domain, path, expires, httpOnly, secure, sameSite)

httpOnly.description: Whether the cookie is HTTP only

name: browser_localstorage_get
description: Get a localStorage item by key

name: browser_sessionstorage_get
description: Get a sessionStorage item by key

name: browser_set_storage_state
description: Restore storage state (cookies, local storage) from a file. This clears existing cookies and local storage before restoring.

filename.description: Path to the storage state file to restore from

name: browser_storage_state
description: Save storage state (cookies, local storage) to a file for later reuse

filename.description: File name to save the storage state to. Defaults to `storage-state-{timestamp}.json` if not specified.

name: browser_mouse_click_xy
description: Click mouse button at a given position

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

delay.description: Time to wait between mouse down and mouse up in milliseconds, defaults to 0

button.description: Button to press, defaults to left

name: browser_mouse_drag_xy
description: Drag left mouse button to a given position

name: browser_mouse_move_xy
description: Move mouse to a given position

button.description: Button to press, defaults to left

filename.description: File name to save the pdf to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.pdf` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

name: browser_generate_locator
description: Generate locator for the given element to use in tests

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available

name: browser_verify_element_visible
description: Verify element is visible on the page

role.description: ROLE of the element. Can be found in the snapshot like this: `- {ROLE} "Accessible Name":`

accessibleName.description: ACCESSIBLE_NAME of the element. Can be found in the snapshot like this: `- role "{ACCESSIBLE_NAME}"`

name: browser_verify_list_visible
description: Verify list is visible on the page

ref.description: Exact target element reference that points to the list

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target list, when "ref" is not available.

name: browser_verify_text_visible
description: Verify text is visible on the page. Prefer browser_verify_element_visible if possible.

text.description: TEXT to verify. Can be found in the snapshot like this: `- role "Accessible Name": {TEXT}` or like this: `- text: {TEXT}`

ref.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot

selector.description: CSS or role selector for the target element, when "ref" is not available

value.description: Value to verify. For checkbox, use "true" or "false".
